M-068.NAME: paid-tariff-catalog
M-068.path: backend/app/billing backend/app/core/migrations.py backend/alembic/versions/phase50_paid_tariff_catalog.py frontend/src/pages/Subscription.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Plans.tsx scripts/phase50-tariff-catalog-smoke.mjs scripts/phase53-user-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs README.md docs/plans/Phase-50.xml docs/plans/Phase-53.xml
M-068.owns: Canonical three-plan paid tariff catalog, server-authoritative payment amounts, device-limit checkout guards, and compact tariff presentation in user/admin frontends.
M-068.purpose: Guarantee that clean deployments and existing deployments converge to the approved KrotPN paid tariff matrix without trusting frontend-provided prices.

exports.fn-canonical-tariff-catalog: Return the approved paid tariff definitions: 1 device for 369 RUB, 6 devices for 693 RUB, and 9 devices for 936 RUB.
exports.fn-upsert-canonical-tariffs: Idempotently create or update canonical plan rows without duplicating plans on backend restart.
exports.fn-validate-checkout-plan: Validate that a selected plan is active, canonical, and compatible with the user's current consumed device slots before payment creation.
exports.ui-user-tariff-cards: Render exactly three compact user-facing tariff cards with price, duration, device limit, and checkout action.
exports.ui-admin-tariff-matrix: Render the canonical tariff matrix in admin panel without exposing accidental delete/edit paths for canonical plans.
exports.smoke-phase50-tariff-catalog: Static smoke for user/admin tariff copy, canonical price/device-limit values, and protected deploy/install surfaces.

constraints.constraint-1: Frontend may submit only plan_id; backend must derive price, currency, duration_days, device_limit, description, and YooKassa metadata from server-side canonical state.
constraints.constraint-2: Clean deploys and backend restarts must not create duplicate plan rows or silently leave stale canonical prices active.
constraints.constraint-3: Purchasing a lower device-limit tariff must be blocked when active or blocked device slots already exceed the target limit.
constraints.constraint-4: Pending four-day trial behavior from M-063 remains unchanged; successful paid checkout cancels pending trial and activates paid access immediately after payment webhook.
constraints.constraint-5: Personal MTProto proxy entitlement remains verified-email based and is not moved behind paid tariffs in Phase-50.
constraints.constraint-6: Do not modify install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, nginx routing, TLS material, VPN runtime topology, or MTProto runtime topology.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: Public billing plans API returns exactly the three active canonical tariffs in sort order with 30-day duration, RUB currency, and device limits 1, 6, and 9.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: Payment creation and YooKassa payload use backend-derived amounts 369.00, 693.00, and 936.00 and include non-secret tariff metadata.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Device provisioning enforces the purchased plan limit and checkout blocks incompatible downgrades before payment creation.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: User and admin frontends build and show compact, Russian, mobile-safe tariff surfaces with no stale placeholder CRUD wording.
acceptance-metrics.metric-5: Backend tariff, billing, device-limit, migration, frontend build, admin build, static smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected deploy/install diff guard pass.
acceptance-metrics.metric-6: Phase-50 verification design is complete before execution: deterministic assertions, trace markers, UI/API contract, and protected-surface guard are explicitly mapped in V-M-068.
acceptance-metrics.metric-7: Phase-53 smoke confirms Matrix subscription UI preserves canonical tariff slugs, server-authoritative checkout shape, and mobile readability.

M-068.evidence: 2026-06-03: Phase-53 frontend build and smoke passed for Matrix tariff UI while preserving backend canonical 369/693/936 RUB prices, 1/6/9 device limits, and plan_id-only checkout shape.
M-068.hotspot: H-055: Paid tariff catalog must keep server-authoritative prices, canonical device limits, no duplicate clean-deploy plans, and no deploy/install drift
